post.description: Creates a new issue and optionally starts an automated investigation. The issue is created from the provided details, with a seed generated internally. Investigation runs asynchronously - poll GET /issues/{id} to check status.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of issues for the authenticated organization

parameters.name: startingAfter
parameters.description: Cursor for forward pagination (ID/UUID to start after)

parameters.name: endingBefore
parameters.description: Cursor for backward pagination (ID/UUID to end before)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search issues by title or description (case-insensitive substring match)

get.description: Lightweight endpoint to retrieve basic issue information by issue number
Retrieves detailed information about a specific issue by its ID (UUID)

patch.description: Updates the status of an existing issue by its ID (UUID)

delete.description: Permanently deletes an issue and all associated data

get.description: Retrieves detailed investigation results including root cause analysis and remediation steps

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Issue version for workflow results (defaults to latest)

get.description: Fetches multiple evidential artifacts by their IDs. Supports up to 100 IDs per request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Artifact IDs to retrieve (can be specified multiple times: ?id=foo&id=bar)

post.description: Sends a question to Antimetal's AI agent and returns a natural language answer. Pass the returned conversation_id in subsequent requests to continue the conversation. Set stream: true (and Accept: text/event-stream) to receive a Server-Sent Events stream.
post.summary: Ask Antimetal's AI agent a question

info.title: Antimetal External API
info.description: Public-facing API for external integrations with Antimetal. Provides programmatic access to issue investigation, results, artifacts, and chat functionality.

environment.description: Environment where the issue occurred (e.g., production, staging)

severity.description: Issue severity level (defaults to MEDIUM if not specified)

triggeredAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the issue was triggered (defaults to current time)

context.description: Additional context metadata for enriching the investigation

alertUrl.description: URL to the alert or incident in the source system (e.g., Datadog, Sentry)

service.description: Service or application name where the issue occurred

errorMessage.description: Error message or stack trace excerpt

host.description: Hostname or instance ID where the issue occurred

createdAt.description: Date and time the issue was created

updatedAt.description: Date and time the issue was last updated

triggeredAt.description: Timestamp when the issue was triggered

latestVersion.description: Most recent version of this issue

request_id.description: Request correlation ID for tracing and support

seedCount.description: Number of seeds for this issue

createdAt.description: Date and time the issue was created

updatedAt.description: Date and time the issue was last updated

seedCount.description: Number of seeds for this issue

link.description: Optional link related to the seed

query.description: Optional query related to the seed

rawPayload.description: Raw payload data from the provider

triggeredAt.description: Date and time the seed was triggered

latestVersion.description: Most recent version of this issue

description.description: Description of the changes in the version

status.description: The new status for the issue

dataType.description: Type of data in this artifact

relevantEvidence.description: Relevant evidence supporting the root cause

dataType.description: Type of data in this artifact

irrelevantEvidence.description: Irrelevant evidence ruled out during investigation

type.description: Node type indicating its state in the causal analysis

expandedContent.description: Optional rich text content in Markdown format providing detailed explanation

id.description: Unique identifier for this node in the causal graph (causal variable) e.g. db_connection_error or deployment_change.

title.description: Brief display name for the causal variable. Use active verbs to show causal flow. CAUSE nodes: action-focused (e.g., 'Flag Activates Bad Store Routing', 'Deployment Introduces Memory Leak'). MEDIATOR nodes: consequence-focused (e.g., 'Requests Routed to Invalid Endpoint', 'Memory Exhaustion Occurs'). OUTCOME nodes: consequence-focused (e.g., 'Service Errors Exceed Threshold', 'Response Times Degrade Beyond SLA'). Keep under 8 words.

description.description: Succinct explanation (1-2 sentences, ~30 words) describing what the underlying evidence shows and the node's role in the incident. Use technical precision: include service names, error codes, field names, file paths. Use direct factual statements without explanations. Skip discovery details - focus on what happened, not how it was found. Include causal mechanism if relevant. Example: 'Configuration shows paymentFailure flag enabled at 12.5% rate, implementing probabilistic failure injection via Math.random() in charge.js:37.' Let evidence documents contain detailed data.

confidence.description: Confidence in this node's role in the incident (e.g., how certain we are that this factor accurately represents what happened)

nodeType.description: Role in the causal graph: OUTCOME = the problem you're investigating, CAUSE = what triggered it, CONFOUNDER = external factor affecting multiple variables, MEDIATOR = intermediate step in the causal path

documentId.description: Unique identifier for the document that contains this evidence (e.g., 'log:datadog:a9c1319258284740a7e5704134d90238', 'trace:gcp:abc123', 'file:github:24192d3b6c1292fd29da76ada910c175')

title.description: Human-readable name for this piece of evidence (e.g., 'Unbounded Cache Growth in recommendation_server.py', 'Deployment memory limit configuration','Deploy Script v2.1')

description.description: Direct factual statement about what the evidence shows and why it's relevant to the causal relationship (e.g., 'Shows 300% CPU spike at 14:23, correlating with error rate increase')

dataType.description: Type of artifact extracted from documentId (log, metric, trace, file, etc.)

from.description: Unique identifier for the source node in the causal graph.

to.description: Unique identifier for the target node in the causal graph.

confidence.description: Edge confidence reflects how certain we are about the causal relationship itself (e.g. how confident we are that the deployment caused the memory leak).

causalTreeV2.description: Causal graph V2 with enriched evidence

category.description: Remediation category: immediate (quick stabilization), root_cause (fixes underlying issue), preventative (prevents recurrence)

files.description: Array of code files to modify

markdownPrompt.description: Markdown prompt describing the suggested steps

content.description: The text content of the file

language.description: The programming language of the code

lineStart.description: The start line of the code snippet, inclusive

lineEnd.description: The end line of the code snippet, inclusive

question.description: Natural language question to ask the AI agent

conversation_id.description: Conversation ID from a previous query response — pass to continue that conversation

stream.description: When true, returns a Server-Sent Events stream. Must also send Accept: text/event-stream header.

conversation_id.description: Pass in subsequent requests to continue this conversation

answer.description: The AI agent's natural language answer

tool_name.description: Name of the tool that produced this artifact

data_type.description: Artifact data type: metric, log, trace, file, event, topology

artifacts.description: Artifacts (metrics, logs, traces, etc.) the agent found while answering

text.description: Text delta — concatenate these to build the full answer

conversation_id.description: Emitted in the final chunk — the conversation ID for follow-up requests

artifact.description: Emitted when a tool produces an artifact

tool_name.description: Name of the tool that produced this artifact

data_type.description: Artifact data type: metric, log, trace, file, event, topology

bearer.description: API key authentication via Bearer token

AntimetalVersion.name: Antimetal-Version
AntimetalVersion.description: The API version to use. Defaults to the latest stable version if omitted. Pin to the version string in this document's `info.version` field to opt into exactly this version's schema.
